title: Daily Market Digest
subtitle: Market Recap for May 30, 2026 (Last Trading Day)

items.headline: U.S. Stocks Close May on High Note, Driven by Tech and AI Optimism
items.summary: U.S. major indices concluded the week and month at record highs on Friday, May 30, 2026, extending a strong rally primarily led by the technology sector and sustained optimism around artificial intelligence. The S&P 500 notched its ninth consecutive weekly gain, the longest such streak since 2023. Despite this bullish momentum, underlying concerns about persistent inflation and geopolitical tensions, particularly the U.S. war with Iran, continued to temper broader market sentiment. Economic indicators showed consumer sentiment at a low, and the 'Buffett indicator' reached an all-time high of around 231%, suggesting a potentially overvalued market.

items.headline: Dell Technologies
items.summary: Surged after reporting profits that significantly exceeded expectations and raising its outlook due to robust demand for AI computing.

items.headline: Astrotech
items.summary: Shares climbed, completing its best week on record following an announcement of plans to focus on lunar-based research and infrastructure development.

items.headline: Microsoft
items.summary: Rose as technology stocks continued to power the market higher amid the AI-led rally.

items.headline: AST SpaceMobile
items.summary: Fell after analysts raised concerns that the explosion of Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket could delay the company's launch targets for the year.

items.headline: Costco Wholesale
items.summary: Closed lower amidst a day where most non-tech sectors experienced declines.

items.headline: Technology Leads as Other Sectors Lag
items.summary: The technology sector continued its robust performance, with tech stocks within the S&P 500 rising over 15% in May, making it the second-best performing sector year-to-date behind energy (as of May 23). This surge is largely attributed to the ongoing AI rally. Conversely, most other sectors in the S&P 500 saw declines on Friday. For the week ending May 29, energy and consumer defensives were among the worst performers, down 5.31% and 3.09% respectively. Financials also saw a weekly decline of 0.69%.

items.headline: Inflation Remains Elevated, Fed Under Scrutiny
items.summary: The Federal Reserve's preferred inflation gauge, the Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) Index, rose 3.8% year-over-year in May, with the Core PCE (excluding food and energy) increasing by 3.3%. These figures remain above the Fed's 2% target, prompting discussions about the possibility of future rate hikes, despite the new Fed Chair Kevin Warsh's previous stance for lower rates. Fed Governor Christopher Waller is scheduled to deliver remarks on stablecoins on May 31, 2026. China's PMI indices for May were also a key macroeconomic indicator observed globally.

items.headline: Strong Q1 Earnings Support Market, AI Drives Growth
items.summary: First-quarter 2026 earnings growth is tracking at an impressive 19.3% year-over-year, more than double consensus expectations. Dell Technologies' outstanding earnings report, fueled by powerful demand for AI computing, served as a prime example of the positive corporate performance driving the market. While major corporate reporting was limited on Friday due to the weekend, AI-driven earnings momentum continues to be a significant factor.

items.headline: Macroeconomic Trends: AI, Geopolitics, and Market Valuation
items.summary: AI-driven growth remains a dominant macro trend, significantly influencing market performance and investor attention. However, the ongoing U.S. war with Iran continues to create worries about inflation and its potential impact on economic growth. The 'Buffett indicator,' comparing U.S. GDP and total stock market value, reached an all-time high of around 231% in May, historically signaling potential market overvaluation. Additionally, the Trump administration announced plans to appeal a ruling allowing importers to seek refunds for previously struck-down tariffs.
